title: Non-constant part-select expression
body:
Here is simple code that elaborated fine, but it should not (

> Both msb_expr and lsb_expr shall be constant integer expressions

 [IEEE 1364-2005 5.2.1].).

```
module non_static_range(input a, b, clk, output reg [7:0] q);
always@(posedge clk)
   q[b+1:1] <= a; // part-select expression either 1:1, or 2:1 depends on 'b' value.
endmodule
```

As width of 'q' is not defined, width of RHS expression also can't be defined at elaboration time, thus rules for expression extension/truncation can't be determine.

>  Yosys 0.6+ (git sha1 UNKNOWN, clang 3.4-1ubuntu3 -fPIC -Os)
> 
> -- Executing script file `scripts/yosys.tcl' --
> 1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
>    Parsing Verilog input from `src/rtl/non_static_range.v' to AST representation.
>    Generating RTLIL representation for module`\non_static_range'.
>    Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
> 2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).
> 
> 2.1. Analyzing design hierarchy..
> Top module:  \non_static_range
> 
> 2.2. Analyzing design hierarchy..
> Top module:  \non_static_range
> Removed 0 unused modules.
code_blocks:
module non_static_range(input a, b, clk, output reg [7:0] q);
always@(posedge clk)
   q[b+1:1] <= a; // part-select expression either 1:1, or 2:1 depends on 'b' value.
endmodule
